ISBN&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the
Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your
Faith&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;Sitting at the feet of Rabbi
Jesus&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Spangler&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Ann&quot;},{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Tverberg&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Lois&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2018&quot;]]}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;69&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;page&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}

Spangler and Lois Tverberg, <i>Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the
Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your Faith</i>, Updated edition (Grand
Rapids, Michigan: Zondervan, 2018), 69.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn16">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="file:///I:/My%20Drive/-TKU/Courses/Luke-Acts%20in%20Jewish%20Context%20(BIBL4310)%2008-10%202022/Research%20Paper,%20Darrell%20Wolfe.docx#_ftnref16" name="_ftn16" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[16]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a> <!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;WkbNH0Oh&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;N.
T. Wright and Michael F. Bird, {\\i{}The New Testament in Its World: An Introduction
to the History, Literature, and Theology of the First Christians}
(London\\uc0\\u8239{}: Grand Rapids, MI: SPCK\\uc0\\u8239{}; Zondervan
Academic, 2019), 630\\uc0\\u8211{}32.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;N.
T. Wright and Michael F. Bird, The New Testament in Its World: An Introduction
to the History, Literature, and Theology of the First Christians (London :
Grand Rapids, MI: SPCK ; Zondervan Academic, 2019),
630–32.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:16},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:22,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/MGER9ADM&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:22,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;abstract&quot;:&quot;\&quot;The
New Testament in Its World brings together decades of ground-breaking research,
writing, and teaching into one volume that will open readers' eyes to the
larger world of the New Testament. It presents the New Testament books as
historical, literary, and social phenomena located in the world of Second
Temple Judaism, amidst Greco-Roman politics and culture, and within early
Christianity. \&quot;--&quot;,&quot;call-number&quot;:&quot;BS2330.3 .W[right]&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;London
: Grand Rapids,
MI&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;978-0-310-49930-5&quot;,&quot;note&quot;:&quot;OCLC:
on1090200946&quot;,&quot;number-of-pages&quot;:&quot;987&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;SPCK
; Zondervan Academic&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;London : Grand
Rapids, MI&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;Library of Congress
ISBN&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;The New Testament in its world: an
introduction to the history, literature, and theology of the first
Christians&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;The New Testament in its
world&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Wright&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;N.
T.&quot;},{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Bird&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Michael
F.&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2019&quot;]]}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;630-632&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;page&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->N. T.
Wright and Michael F. Bird, <i>The New Testament in Its World: An Introduction
to the History, Literature, and Theology of the First Christians</i> (London :
Grand Rapids, MI: SPCK ; Zondervan Academic, 2019), 630–32.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

kingdom&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Chilton&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Bruce&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;1996&quot;]]}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;126&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;page&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Bruce
Chilton, <i>Pure Kingdom: Jesus’ Vision of God</i>, Studying the Historical
Jesus (Grand Rapids, Mich. : London: Eerdmans ; Society for Prommoting
Christian Knowledge, 1996), 126.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

faith. This newly expanded softcover edition includes a discussion guide and a

</div><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p class="p13" style="margin-left: 0rem; min-height: 16px; text-indent: 1.5rem;"><b>Darrell Wolfe,&nbsp;<a href="http://www.nohiding.faith/p/blog-page.html" target="_blank">Storyteller at NoHiding.Faith</a></b></p><p class="p13" style="margin-left: 0rem; min-height: 16px; text-indent: 1.5rem;"><br style="font-family: &quot;Sitka Text&quot;; font-size: 16px;" /></p>
